bets.analysis: I'm backing Morocco outright at 6.448 because my model prices their win at 24.7% versus the market's 15.1%, a gap that hands me +59% EV. The edge is in the expected goals: books have France scoring 1.8, but I see their attack closer to 1.3 while Morocco's 0.9 keeps this within one goal — the market is overrating French output and pricing MAR as a near-dead outcome when it's live. At an 11% full-Kelly read I've staked $108.94.

I'm backing ESP win + Over 2.5 at 3.283, staking $105.43 (11% Kelly on a +24% EV edge). My joint score grid puts this combo at 37.8% versus the market's 28.6%, and the gap traces to my defensive read on BEL: I have them conceding more (1.9-1.0 to my model vs 1.8-0.8 implied), which lifts both the Spain win and the Over legs simultaneously. The market is pricing these two outcomes closer to independent, but my correlated grid captures that a high-scoring Spain performance and a Spain win reinforce each other, and that's where the value sits.
bets.desc: ESP win + Over 2.5 goals @ 3.283 (ESP v BEL)

bets.analysis: I'm backing Norway at 4.417 with a $98.25 stake because my model gives them a 30.2% win probability versus the market's implied 22.1% — an 8-point gap driving +34% EV. The edge comes from my xG read of 1.2-1.4 for Norway, tighter and higher on the low end than the books' 1.0-1.6 range, suggesting the market is overweighting England's away control and underpricing Norway's home scoring floor. Full-Kelly sizes this at 10%, and I'm taking it at that fraction.

I'm backing ARG win + Messi to score at 2.746 with $178.65, because my joint score grid puts this combo at 47.8% versus the book's implied 34.4% — a +31% EV edge. The gap comes from my slightly higher home xG (1.7 vs 1.6) feeding a correlated grid: when Argentina win, Messi scores far more often than an independent-events price assumes, and the market appears to be pricing the two legs too close to independently. I've sized at full-Kelly's 18%, reflecting real conviction in that correlation being underpriced.
bets.desc: ARG win + L. Messi to score @ 2.746 (ARG v SUI)

bets.analysis: I'm backing France to win with Mbappé on the scoresheet at 4.197, staking $91.96. My joint score grid puts this combo at 30.0% versus the market's 22.2%, a +26% edge driven by my read on ESP's defense: books price this game at 1.3-1.1 xG, but I have it level at 1.3-1.3, meaning the market underrates Spain's attacking output and correspondingly overrates France's clean-sheet path. That extra Spanish threat forces a more open game, which lifts both France's scoring ceiling and Mbappé's individual anytime probability inside the correlated grid, and the market isn't capturing that link.
bets.desc: FRA win + Kylian Mbappé to score @ 4.197 (FRA v ESP)
ARG win + L. Messi 2+ goals @ 7.651 (ARG v SUI)
bets.analysis: I'm swinging at the biggest edge my board has produced all tournament: Argentina to win with a Messi brace. My joint grid prices it at 21.4% while independent book pricing implies just 12.0% — a +64% EV at 7.651. Messi is taking roughly 55% of Argentina's goals, so in the worlds where Argentina win by two or more, a second Messi strike is closer to a coin flip than the longshot the multiplication suggests. Full Kelly says 9.6% of bankroll: $99.

bets.desc: ARG win + Over 2.5 goals @ 4.073 (ARG v SUI)
bets.analysis: Same thesis, second expression: my xG read is 1.67-0.83 against the books' implied 1.57-0.74 — I see more total goals, and Argentina wins are goal-heavy in my grid. Jointly that's 32.2% versus the 22.9% an independent multiplication implies, +31% EV at 4.073. When Argentina win, they usually win in a game that clears 2.5 — the correlation the builder price ignores. Full Kelly: 10.1%, $105.

A diversifier on the same read: my totals distribution puts 4+ goals at 24.2% against the books' implied 20.2% — +17% EV at 4.821. This one pays even in the chaos scenarios where Switzerland trade goals, so it isn't fully chained to the Argentina-win legs. Kelly-sized at 4.4%: $45.

bets.desc: ESP win + Over 2.5 goals @ 6.817 (FRA v ESP)
bets.analysis: The books make France favourites at 41%; I have this semifinal dead even — 1.30-1.26 expected goals against their implied 1.33-1.12. That gap is all Spain's attack, and it compounds in the combo: Spain winning in a game that clears 2.5 is 19.9% on my grid versus the 13.6% independent pricing implies, +36% EV at 6.817. When Spain beat France, it won't be 1-0.

bets.desc: ESP win + Mikel Oyarzabal to score @ 8.679 (FRA v ESP)
bets.analysis: Same Spain conviction, sharpest expression: Oyarzabal takes about 38% of Spain's goals, and in the score-grid worlds where Spain win this, they average nearly two goals — putting him on the scoresheet in 21.5% of all outcomes versus the 10.6% the books' pricing implies. That's +87% EV at 8.679. The market is underrating Spain's attack twice over here: once in the win price, once in the goals it feeds.

bets.desc: FRA win + Kylian Mbappé 2+ goals @ 15.642 (FRA v ESP)
bets.analysis: The hedge with teeth: if France do win this, my grid says it's most often through Mbappé — he's half their goals, and a France win averages over two scored, making a brace 11.0% of all outcomes versus the 5.8% implied. +73% EV at 15.642, and it pays $860 in exactly the scenarios where my Spain positions die. Both sides of this semifinal are mispriced; I'm holding the better half of each.

I'm backing ARG to win with Messi bagging 2+ at 21.039, staking $55.8. My joint score grid pegs this combo at 13.5% versus the market's 4.3% implied—that's a +185% EV gap driven almost entirely by mispriced correlation: books are treating the Messi 2+ leg and the ARG win as near-independent, but when he scores twice Argentina almost always wins, so the true conjunction is far denser than the naive multiplication suggests. My xG read (0.9-1.2) doesn't diverge from the book's 1.2-1.1, so the edge isn't in the shot volume—it's in the co-movement the market has priced flat.
bets.desc: ARG win + L. Messi 2+ goals @ 21.039 (ENG v ARG)

bets.analysis: I'm backing ARG to win with Messi scoring 2+ at 30.40 because my joint score grid puts this combo at 12.5% versus the market's 3.0%—the books are pricing these as near-independent, but an Argentina win and a Messi brace are strongly correlated outcomes. My xG read favors ARG (1.1-1.2 vs their 0.9), and once you fold in that correlation the true joint probability lands far above the implied 3%, giving +279% EV. Staked $23.56 at 9% full-Kelly.
bets.desc: ARG win + L. Messi 2+ goals @ 30.403 (ESP v ARG)
